task_name: Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery - Overview Presentation

frame_analyses.description: Blue gradient background with no FLS task content visible
Title slide: 'Why?' - Lists reasons for FLS (ready-made training module, validated assessment, ABS mandate, credentials, self-learning)
Title slide: 'Who?' - Describes target audience (residents, fellows, teaching faculty, community surgeons, GYN, pediatric surgery, urology)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content explaining FLS program scope

frame_analyses.description: Continuation of 'Who?' slide with same content
Title slide: 'Universality' - Explains FLS as non-procedure based, fundamental knowledge/skill, not for experts, reviewed by GYN and Pediatric Surgery, being validated as GYN competency tool
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on program universality and validation

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'When?' - Covers FLS as training tool (resident training, skills acquisition, graduation certification) and competency measurement (credentialing, risk management)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on timing and applications

frame_analyses.description: Continuation of 'When?' slide with same content

Title slide: 'Suggested 5 year program curricula' - Year 1 and 2 details (didactic sessions 1hr q 2 weeks, 1 hour manual skills training FLS box peg transfer; Year 3 formal FLS manual skills sessions 2X month; Year 4 self study online/web, testing day with didactic and test sessions)
Continuation of suggested 5 year program curricula slide

Title slide: 'Resident Curricula 2' - Details for junior residents year 1 (16 hr FLS didactic slide set, 1hr/2x month), Year 2 and 3 skills training (FLS box proctored 1 hr/month, required self study 1 hr/month, peg transfer, suturing, etc, FLS tasks $student per year), Year 4 (Afternoon FLS cognitive study 4 hr slide set, manual skills practice session 1 hour, FLS proctored testing 2.5 hours/resident)
Title slide: 'Where?' - Map of North America showing 61 test centers with stars indicating locations across USA, Canada, and border regions. Lists specific testing centers by state.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on geographic distribution of FLS testing centers

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'FLS Testing' - Lists 61 test centers in North America and counting, detailed list of testing center locations, SAGES and ACS Annual Meetings, Australia and New Zealand testing options
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on testing availability and locations

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'At your institution!' - Individual Package (Self study and test center voucher), Education Packages (A - Online Access 3 vouchers; B - Online Access 5 Vouchers Trainer Box; C - Online Access 5 Vouchers Trainer Box on-site testing proctor)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on FLS purchasing/implementation options

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'How?' - Online (www.flsprogram.org, www.SAGES.org), By phone (310) 437-0544, By email (fls@sages.org), On site (FLS Postgraduate Courses SAGES April 2010 Washington DC, ACS Clinical Congress October 2010 Washington DC)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content on how to access FLS program

frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Additional questions?' - Closing slide with background image of surgeons at laparoscopic trainer

confidence_rationale: Video is classified as instructional educational material, not a performance or expert demonstration. It is a PowerPoint-style presentation about the FLS program overview, including why it exists, who should take it, when it should be integrated into training, where testing centers are located, and how to access the program. No actual FLS task performance is shown.
technique_summary: Not applicable — this is educational/informational content, not a task performance video.

verbal_cues: FLS designed for 'any surgeon who uses the laparoscope'
Non-procedure based fundamental knowledge and skill
Results expected for a 'minimally acceptable' laparoscopist
Currently being validated as GYN competency measuring tool
key_topics_covered: Target audience (residents, fellows, faculty, community surgeons, GYN, pediatric, urology)
Testing center distribution (61 centers in North America)
